_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: LOCAL closeout batch — zachary-parker CLOSES the DC LOCAL set (13/13 DC councilmembers + Mayor now evidence-scored: Allen + Bowser + Crawford + Felder + Frumin + Henderson + Lewis-George + Mendelson + Parker + Pinto[prior] + R.White + T.White + plus McDuffie[former→federal]). Parker = 6th DC archetype-5 catch (gun-manufacturer-liability AUTHOR + DSA Defund-MPD-WG meeting + Stonewall/GLAA endorsed + RCCA-override YES + standalone-LGBTQ-Affairs-office proposal + failed deed-tax author + DCPS-only SBOE-presidency roots); committee assignment correction = Youth Affairs chair (DYRS oversight), NOT Education (repo's prior prediction wrong on committee, right on archetype). Plus 4 Brevard County FL constitutional officers: dana-blickley (Property Appraiser, roster_verified, 4th unopposed term, single intra-GOP Tobia transparency dispute single-source-partisan = honest-NULL not scored), lisa-cullen (Tax Collector, roster_verified, 38-yr career, 2024 first contested vs Pritchett, sole on-record position = oppose-term-limits-for-constitutional-officers self-interest = NOT scoreable on RESOLUTE rubric), rachel-sadoff (Clerk of Circuit Court & Comptroller, evidence_local HARD-TRUE econ_q2 = 2022 Lober p-card audit 'indiscriminate, wasteful, and not for a legitimate public use' + FDLE referral; Catholic proxy via St Joseph Catholic School board + Barry MPA; first female Brevard Clerk in 167 years), thad-altman (BoCC D5 since Jan 2025 + 30+ years FL state-leg record: HB 5 2022 15-wk abortion ban YES + HB 1429 2018 dismemberment-ban YES + HB 1557 2022 Parental Rights YES + HB 89 2018 E-Verify LEAD SPONSOR + HB 1B Nov 2021 anti-mandate Special Session YES as TRUE-cluster vs HB 1 2023 universal-voucher NO + SB 7026 2018 Parkland red-flag/age-21 YES + publicly DEFENDED as FALSE-cluster; Catholic per Vote Smart; Freedom Index 67-83%; endorsed 'Trump Republican' Terry Cronin March 2025). DC consistency rule: refuse_state_overreach all NULL for Parker (DC is not a state). FL constitutional officers Blickley + Cullen scored roster_verified per LOCAL skill admin-only protocol (no findable public political positions outside statutory office duties).

profile.background: DC Council Ward 5 since January 2 2023 (succeeded Kenyan McDuffie). First Black LGBTQ+ DC Councilmember (self-identified as 'a proud Black gay man' March 2022). Chairs Committee on Youth Affairs (DYRS oversight). Former DC State Board of Education Ward 5 representative 2018-2022; immediate-past SBOE President. Endorsed by Capital Stonewall Democrats, Metro DC DSA, GLAA, and Greater Greater Washington. Filed for June 16 2026 D primary re-election; campaign site zacharyparker2026.com. Signature 25th-Council bills: Establishment of Reasonable Controls for the Firearm Industry Act (gun-manufacturer-liability — enables DC OAG + private residents to sue gun makers); School Improvement Amendment Act (expanding support for low-performing public schools); Prep DC Act of 2025 (expanding HIV-medication access); failed FY25 deed-tax (mansion-tax-style transfer-tax hike — died at first budget vote); District Child Tax Credit (refundable credit for families with kids ≤17). Voted YES on RCCA override Jan 17 2023 (12-1) with sentencing-equity framing; voted YES on Secure DC final passage with harm-reduction reservation language at first reading.

zachary-parker.notes_set: DC archetype-5 CLOSEOUT — Parker brings the DC LOCAL archetype-5 cluster to 6 (Allen + Crawford + Lewis-George + R.White + Frumin + Parker) and CLOSES the DC LOCAL set (13/13 Mayor+Councilmembers evidence-scored). HARD-FALSE PUBLIC JUSTICE CLUSTER: (1) PJ q4 gun-manufacturer-liability AUTHOR — 'Establishment of Reasonable Controls for the Firearm Industry Act' enables DC OAG + private citizens to sue gun manufacturers, framed as 'measured accountability for the ongoing trauma gun violence inflicts'; (2) PJ q0 DSA Defund-MPD Working Group meeting attendance during 2022 Ward 5 campaign + Metro DC DSA endorsement + Metro Weekly 'root causes through education, jobs, and recreation rather than reactive approaches' quote; (3) PJ q1 Secure DC YES on final passage but with skeptical 'what stops them from going down the street?' drug-free-zone framing at first reading + post-passage harm-reduction explanatory note on his official site — clears Secure DC YES but not pro-MPD-empowerment POSTURE. ECON q2 FALSE — failed deed-tax (mansion-tax) author + YES on Mendelson FY25/FY26 sales+property tax hike packages. BM q0/q2/q4 FALSE — first Black LGBTQ+ DC councilmember (self-ID) + Washington Blade 'supports advancing transgender healthcare rights' + standalone-LGBTQ-Affairs-office proposal + 2026 Pride flag raising attendance + Stonewall/GLAA endorsements. FAM q0 FALSE — DCPS-only SBOE-presidency roots + School Improvement Amendment Act (public-only). FAM q1 FALSE — Prep DC Act 2025 expanding minor HIV-medication access without parental-consent carve-out. BI q2 FALSE — Council majority that blocked Bowser's May 2025 Sanctuary Values Act repeal; no Parker dissent. SD q0/q1 FALSE — gun-mfr-liability AUTHOR. Committee assignment CORRECTION: Youth Affairs chair (DYRS oversight), NOT Education (repo's prior commit predicted 'Education chair' — wrong on committee, right on archetype-5 classification). Religion NULL — not self-identified publicly after searching Wikipedia, Ballotpedia, Metro Weekly 12-Questions, Washington Blade, campaign site. DC consistency: refuse_state_overreach all NULL (DC is not a state — same pattern as prior 10 DC officials).

0.note: No personhood-from-conception statement located after searching campaign site, X @CMZParker5, Metro Weekly 12-Questions interview, Washington Blade coverage, DC Council member page. Honest NULL — Stonewall/GLAA endorsement chain implies pro-choice alignment but no direct quote located.

1.note: Not seated for Law 24-254 (Enhancing Reproductive Health Protections, Oct 2022) or Law 24-257 (Human Rights Sanctuary, Oct 2022) — sworn Jan 2 2023. No direct abortion-related vote or co-sponsorship located during his 25th Council tenure.

0.note: First Black LGBTQ+ DC Councilmember; self-identified March 2022 as 'a proud Black gay man.'

2.note: Washington Blade May 2024: Parker 'supports advancing transgender healthcare rights' as part of standalone-LGBTQ-Affairs-agency proposal.

4.note: Proposed elevating Mayor's Office of LGBTQ Affairs to standalone agency; attended 2026 DC Pride flag-raising; speaker at 2025 International LGBTQ+ Leaders Conference; Stonewall (64.5% first-ballot 2022) + GLAA endorsements.

0.note: DCPS-only roots: DC State Board of Education Ward 5 rep 2018-2022 + immediate-past SBOE President. 25th-Council signature 'School Improvement Amendment Act' explicitly expands support for low-performing PUBLIC schools; no DC Opportunity Scholarship voucher endorsement located, contrary to Mendelson's 2015 reauth stance.

1.note: Authored Prep DC Act of 2025 expanding minor access to HIV-prevention medication without documented parental-consent carve-out; consistent with DC's Minor Consent to Vaccinations Act 2020 framework Parker has not opposed.

2.note: No direct DC Health Education standards / CRT-SOGI vote located during 25th Council. Honest NULL — DSA + Stonewall coalition implies SOGI-curriculum alignment but no direct vote sourced.

3.note: No public-square Christian symbols / Sabbath record located.

4.note: No state-funded non-Christian display vote located.

2.note: FY25 'deed tax' (mansion-tax-style transfer-tax hike) AUTHOR — proposal died at first budget vote; consistent YES votes on Mendelson-chaired FY25/FY26 sales+property tax hike packages. District Child Tax Credit author (refundable credit, expanding entitlement). No fiscal-restraint posture located.

1.note: DC has no voter-ID requirement; Parker has not introduced one and is in the progressive coalition that opposes them.

4.note: Not seated for Law 24-242 (Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act / noncitizen voting) Oct/Nov 2022 readings; sworn Jan 2 2023. No noncitizen-voting repeal advocacy located; no Zuckerbucks stance located.

0.note: 2022 Ward 5 campaign: attended Metro DC DSA 'Defund MPD Working Group' meeting; received Metro DC DSA endorsement; Metro Weekly 12-Questions: advocated addressing 'root causes of violence through education, jobs, and recreation rather than reactive approaches.' No 'fully fund MPD' statement located. HIGH BAR: did NOT publicly back police affirmatively.

1.note: Secure DC first-reading skeptical posture: 'If you establish a drug-free zone, in a certain intersection or community, what stops them from going down the street?' Voted YES on final passage but framed post-passage explanatory note in harm-reduction terms — not pro-accountability/proportional-punishment terms.

2.note: Root-causes-not-punishment framing; no proportional-restitution posture located.

3.note: No clear public-health-vs-punishment statement; harm-reduction framing is suggestive but not direct on q3.

4.note: Authored 'Establishment of Reasonable Controls for the Firearm Industry Act' enabling DC OAG + private residents to sue gun manufacturers: 'By empowering the Office of the Attorney General and private citizens to sue gun industry members for harms caused by a failure to implement these reasonable controls, we can take a meaningful step towards measured accountability for the ongoing trauma gun violence inflicts on District residents.' Opposite of magistrate-bears-sword posture.

profile.background: Brevard County Property Appraiser since November 2012 (sworn January 2013). Re-elected unopposed in 2016, 2020, and 2024 — now serving 4th consecutive 4-year term (through January 2029). Career Brevard County Property Appraiser's Office: 30+ years mass appraisal experience; Certified Florida Appraiser (CFA) designation; former Director of Tax Roll and CAMA Systems at BCPAO before election. Office handles statutory ad valorem assessment, homestead/agricultural/TPP exemptions, Value Adjustment Board defense, TRIM recommendations; does NOT set millage rates or pass policy. Salary ~$184,356.

dana-blickley.notes_set: ROSTER_VERIFIED — admin-only constitutional officer. After thorough search of BCPAO official site, dormant @ElectDana Twitter, BCPAO Facebook, LinkedIn, Brevard County GOP leadership list (Dittmore Chair/Fosdick/Twiddy/Stackhouse — Blickley NOT a REC officer), FloridaPolitics, FloridaToday, Space Coast Daily, Voterfocus campaign finance (latest cycle: $0 itemized contributions / $0 in-kind / $0 expenditures — consistent with unopposed status), Dean Mead TRIM seminars, and Titusville Chamber appearances, no findable public political positions on Trump/DeSantis/Ivey/school-board endorsements, Pride, abortion, marriage, election integrity, sanctuary-county, RCV, CBDC, COVID mandates, HB 1557, or ESG. Religion not locatable. SINGLE controversy data-point: June 28 2023 Commissioner John Tobia (R, Dist 3) public press release alleging BCPAO withheld FY2023-24 budget line-item detail (employee travel, postage, vehicle inventory, mailings, data-processing purchases, reduction-request justifications) from the Brevard County Commission in violation of FL Stat. 195.087(1)(a). Carried by partisan publisher Space Coast Rocket; Blickley acknowledged asymmetric disclosure at June 28 Special Meeting; no actual ethics complaint filed; no FDLE/IG referral; no adjudicated finding. Honest-NULL on rubric — single-source intra-GOP unadjudicated dispute does not clear the evidence bar for an economic_stewardship FALSE. All rubric cells NULL per LOCAL skill admin-only constitutional-officer protocol.

profile.background: Brevard County Tax Collector since January 2009 (assumed office after 2008 election). Re-elected unopposed in 2012, 2016, 2020. 2024 was her first contested race: defeated term-limited County Commissioner Rita Pritchett (R, North Brevard District 1) in August 20 2024 Republican primary; write-in candidate Daniel Lopez (New Life Space Coast Church youth minister) qualified to close the GOP primary then withdrew before November general, handing Cullen the seat outright per ClickOrlando Sept 4 2024 reporting. Now serving through 2028. Career office: began as deputy clerk 1985 at age 19; Certified Florida Collector Assistant Nov 1994-Feb 2009; CFC certification Feb 2009-present. UCF bachelor's; Eastern Florida State College; Merritt Island HS; born Logan WV. Office responsibilities: collect ad valorem property taxes, vehicle/vessel/mobile-home titles + tags, hunting/fishing licenses, business tax receipts, concealed-weapons-license processing on behalf of FDACS, tourist development tax. Does not set rates or policy.

lisa-cullen.notes_set: ROSTER_VERIFIED — admin-only constitutional officer. 38-year career deputy-to-Collector trajectory. 2024 campaign endorsements (Business Voice PC + Space Coast Realtors + Home Builders & Contractors Assoc + conservative-talk-radio host Bill Mick) are business/industry-conservative, not Christian-conservative coalition. Sole substantive policy position on record: opposed Commissioner Pritchett's February 2024 proposal to limit constitutional officers to three 4-year terms (motion died for lack of a second); Cullen's stated rationale: 'a state legislator doesn't have to oversee a staff of 207 like myself. This is not an easy job to step into.' This is a self-interested institutional-incumbency stance, not a RESOLUTE-rubric-scoreable position. FY24 issue-platform per FloridaToday questionnaire focused on reopening Indian Harbour Beach office post-COVID + streamlining online services — pure administrative agenda, zero ideological content. No public 2A statements located (despite FL HB 543 / July 2023 Constitutional Carry changing CWL landscape — Tax Collectors retained customer-service role for FDACS-issued permits, no Cullen reaction located). No Christian / pro-life / marriage / Pride-opposition / school-board / Trump / DeSantis / election-integrity statements located. Personal Facebook is 'Lisa Cullen for Tax Collector' campaign page; institutional office Facebook is @brevardtax. No personal Twitter located. No Brevard REC officer role. No church affiliation locatable. Religion not locatable. Notably the church-angle in the 2024 race attached to OPPONENT Pritchett (administrator/radio host at New Life Space Coast Church) and write-in Lopez (youth minister there), NOT to Cullen. All rubric cells NULL per LOCAL skill admin-only constitutional-officer protocol.

set.office: Clerk of the Circuit Court and Comptroller, Brevard County

profile.religion: Catholic (proxy: Saint Joseph Catholic School board service + Barry University MPA — not self-declared)
profile.background: Brevard County Clerk of the Circuit Court and Comptroller since January 1 2021 (sworn ad interim Jan 1 2021 after predecessor Scott Ellis resigned Dec 31 2020; officially sworn Jan 5 2021). First female Brevard Clerk in 167 years. Dual-hat role: Clerk + County Comptroller + Auditor + County Recorder. Career deputy clerk: started Brevard Clerk's office 1997 under Sandy Crawford; rose to Viera Branch Manager (Viera + Brevard County Jail). First elected 2020 unopposed after Sandra Ruizzo failed to qualify in GOP primary. Re-elected unopposed 2024 through December 31 2028. EFSC (B.A. Professional Studies); Barry University (MPA). Married to Steve Sadoff; three children. Board service: Saint Joseph Catholic School (Palm Bay), Space Coast Marine Institute youth diversion program, Bays Florida. Official bio language: family 'values public service, veterans, and law enforcement.'

rachel-sadoff.notes_set: ARCHETYPE: career-administrator R-comptroller continuing Scott-Ellis-watchdog tradition; SINGLE HARD-TRUE on economic_stewardship q2 for the 2022 Lober purchasing-card audit. Headline finding: After unanimous BoCC request February 22 2022, Sadoff conducted full audit of Commissioner Bryan Lober (R, then-District 2)'s ~$40K p-card spending. Final audit concluded card use 'was often indiscriminate, wasteful, and not for a legitimate public use.' Over $9,000 unaccounted-for items; suspected misuse of tax-exemption certificate; personal email used to evade public-records law. Referred to FDLE for criminal investigation November 2022. When Lober personally requested she NOT audit him, Sadoff publicly disclosed the request: 'I would never let a friendship influence how myself or my office exercises authority under the law' (Space Coast Rocket, 2022). She also rejected suggestion that contracted auditors are preferable to an elected comptroller answerable to voters. Sadoff v. Board of County Commissioners of Brevard County (2022) appellate-court litigation exists per FindLaw — confirming her willingness to take BoCC to court as Comptroller. Stated mission to 'expand upon Clerk Ellis's legacy of transparency, accountability, and accessibility' — Ellis was the famously combative gadfly; Sadoff has delivered. CONSTITUTIONAL-OFFICE ARCHETYPE BREAK: unlike the other 3 Brevard constitutional officers in this batch (Blickley/Cullen/Ivey-prior who are roster_verified-only), Sadoff has produced a named-target, named-amount, public-record fiscal-watchdog action against a fellow Republican commissioner — a real on-record-deviation TRUE on economic_stewardship q2. Biblical marriage q0 honest-NULL: was deputy clerk (Viera branch manager) in 2015 when Obergefell came down, NOT a policymaker; Brevard's then-Clerk Scott Ellis quietly complied (no public refusal incident comparable to Baker Co Stacie Harvey or KY Kim Davis); no Sadoff public statement on Obergefell, same-sex marriage policy, or conscience-refusal located. Catholic-affiliation is proxy-strength (St Joseph Catholic School board + Barry MPA at Adrian-Dominican Catholic institution), not self-declared. Other categories NULL per office-scope + no findable positions.

2.note: DEVIATION (HARD-TRUE in admin-office context): 2022 Lober purchasing-card audit. After unanimous BoCC request Feb 22 2022, Sadoff completed full audit of Commissioner Bryan Lober's ~$40K p-card spending. Audit conclusion: 'often indiscriminate, wasteful, and not for a legitimate public use.' Over $9,000 unaccounted-for items; suspected tax-exemption-certificate misuse; personal-email evasion of public records. Referred to FDLE Nov 2022. When Lober personally asked her not to audit him, Sadoff publicly disclosed: 'I would never let a friendship influence how myself or my office exercises authority under the law.' Named-target, named-amount, public-record fiscal watchdog action against a fellow R Commissioner.

profile.background: Brevard County Commissioner District 5 since January 2025 (now BoCC chair per Wikipedia). 30+ years FL state-legislative experience: FL House 1984-1992, FL House 2003-2008 (D30), FL Senate 2008-2016 (D24/D16), FL House 2016-2024 (D52/D32 successively, term-limited from each chamber). Freedom Index FL scores: 75% (2017-18), 67% (2019-20), 83% (2021-22), 75% (2023-24) — solid but not maximal conservative. Known signature reputation as Kennedy Space Center / space-industry funding advocate and public-records-reform sponsor. Endorsed 'Trump Republican' Terry Cronin for FL House March 2025 (Space Coast Daily). Born September 8 1955. Catholic per Vote Smart.

thad-altman.notes_set: MIXED MODERATE-R PROFILE — 30+ year FL legislator with substantial pro-life + parental-rights + E-Verify signature WITH meaningful red-flag-gun-control + universal-voucher-skeptic deviations. HARD-TRUE CLUSTER (5): (1) sanctity_of_life q0+q1 — HB 5 2022 15-week abortion ban YES + HB 1429 2018 dismemberment-abortion ban YES (consistent pro-life voting); (2) biblical_marriage q2+q4 + family q0+q2 — HB 1557 2022 Parental Rights in Education ('Don't Say Gay') YES (signature parental-rights + biological-sex + SOGI-curriculum-opposition vote); (3) border q2 — HB 89 2018 E-Verify LEAD SPONSOR + anti-sanctuary HB 9 cosponsor (strong border/sanctuary-opposition signature). HARD-FALSE CLUSTER (2 significant deviations): (1) family q1 (school choice) — voted NO on HB 1 2023 universal-voucher expansion per Freedom Index, breaking with DeSantis-era FL GOP consensus on universal school choice (notable break for a long-tenured R); (2) self_defense q0+q1 — voted YES on SB 7026 2018 Parkland Marjory Stoneman Douglas Public Safety Act (raised long-gun age to 21, created red-flag/risk-protection orders, 3-day waiting period) + publicly DEFENDED his support including the red-flag provisions in 2023 Phoenix retrospective. ECON q2 FALSE — career Kennedy Space Center pork-advocacy is his signature legislative legacy (his Wikipedia identifies space-industry funding as defining issue), not budget-restraint. PUBLIC JUSTICE all NULL — no direct 'publicly backed police' affirmative statement located meeting the LOCAL-skill HIGH-BAR requirement (voting a budget that funds police is NOT enough); HB 1 2021 anti-riot individual vote not located. CHRISTIAN LIBERTY all NULL — no specific religious-liberty votes located. ELECTION INTEGRITY q1 NULL — SB 90 2021 and SB 524 2022 specific votes not located individually despite being in House. REFUSE_STATE q0-q4 ALL NULL — only ~5 months in county office; no Brevard BoCC RSO-relevant vote located (Bill of Rights Sanctuary County December 2024 ordinance was pulled by Feltner before vote per Yahoo News; Brevard 2025 millage vote not pulled). State-leg HB 1B Nov 2021 anti-vaccine-mandate Special Session YES vote is REFUSE-FEDERAL not REFUSE-STATE territory — kept honest-NULL on RSO. Catholic religion per Vote Smart bio; Endorsed Terry Cronin March 2025 as 'Trump Republican.' Estimated net /100: ~28-35 — moderate-R archetype with pro-life + HB 1557 + E-Verify TRUE cluster offset by red-flag + universal-voucher-NO + space-pork FALSE deviations.

0.note: Voted YES on HB 5 (2022 15-week abortion ban) and HB 1429 (2018 dismemberment-abortion ban) — consistent pro-life voting across multiple sessions.

1.note: Same: HB 5 2022 + HB 1429 2018 affirmative restrictions on abortion procedures. While 15-week is not personhood-abolition, it is in the affirmative-restriction column the LOCAL rubric treats as advocating against abortion.

0.note: No direct marriage 1-man-1-woman affirmation statement located beyond party affiliation.

2.note: Voted YES on HB 1557 2022 Parental Rights in Education Act — bill restricts classroom instruction on sexual orientation and gender identity K-3 and requires age-appropriate framing thereafter; treats biological sex as the framework for K-12 instruction.

4.note: HB 1557 YES is the signature FL legislative vote opposing LGBTQ+ classroom-identity promotion in public schools.

0.note: DEVIATION: voted NO on HB 1 2023 universal voucher expansion per Freedom Index — broke with DeSantis-era FL GOP consensus on universal school choice. Notable rare-R break that downgrades school-choice cell to FALSE.

1.note: No specific transgender-minor-care / parental-consent-on-medical vote located individually during his tenure.

2.note: HB 1557 Parental Rights in Education YES = textbook opposition to SOGI/CRT-style content in K-12 public schools.

2.note: DEVIATION: Kennedy Space Center / space-industry funding advocacy is his signature legislative legacy per Wikipedia — not budget-restraint posture. Freedom Index 67-83% across sessions reflects this moderate fiscal profile.

1.note: SB 90 2021 and SB 524 2022 individual votes not located despite his House tenure during both periods; FL R caucus generally voted YES but Altman-specific roll-call not pulled. Honest NULL.

4.note: Filed HB 89 (2018) E-Verify employment-verification bill as LEAD SPONSOR — strong pro-citizenship-enforcement signature consistent with opposition to foreign-influenced or noncitizen election participation.

0.note: LOCAL skill HIGH BAR: 'publicly backed police' requires AFFIRMATIVE advocacy, NOT just voting a budget that funds police. No specific affirmative pro-LE statement from Altman located meeting this bar. Honest NULL despite general pro-LE party alignment.

1.note: HB 1 2021 anti-riot individual vote not located (party-line 76-39 suggests YES but not confirmed). Honest NULL.

2.note: 2017 HB 245 Stand-Your-Ground burden-shift vote not located individually.

0.note: Only ~5 months in county office (sworn Jan 2025); no Brevard BoCC vote on county-vs-state mandate located. HB 1B Nov 2021 anti-vaccine-mandate Special Session YES vote was REFUSE-FEDERAL (state legislature resisting federal mandate) not REFUSE-STATE territory — kept honest-NULL on RSO.

1.note: No declined state-strings vote located in his county tenure.

Did not support constitutional-carry posture.
